title: seegeriella crothersii
intro: Seegeriella crothersii, also known as the "Bristly Clubmoss," is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the genus Seegeriella. It is a rare and endangered species of club moss, native to the tropical regions of Australia and New Guinea. The medicinal properties of Seegeriella crothersii include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant has been used in herbal preparations to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions like eczema and wounds, and its health benefits include soothing and calming effects on the body.
